title: no upper bound on Signature header length before parsing
description:
LOCATION: src/signatures.rs:114-170, src/server.rs:770-771

FINDING: parse_signature_header() and the underlying split_signature_params() allocate Vec<String> from the raw Signature header value without enforcing a maximum length on the input. The Signature header is attacker-controlled. A request with a multi-megabyte Signature header bypasses the 256KB body limit (which only applies to the request body, not headers) and causes the parser to allocate large Vec<String> entries before any validation occurs.

IMPACT: An attacker sending requests with extremely large Signature headers can trigger multi-megabyte heap allocations per request, enabling memory exhaustion (OOM) against the server. The inbox rate limiter (60 req/min) limits throughput but does not bound per-request allocation size.
close_reason: Fixed: Signature header rejected if >8192 bytes before parsing
